titlestmt.titleproper: Osvaldo Böhm Photograph Collection: Art and Architecture of Venice
Osvaldo Böhm Photograph Collection: Art and Architecture of Venice
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Robert Carlucci

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 8069018.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday September 1, 2020 at 23:17

did.unittitle: Osvaldo Böhm Photograph Collection: Art and Architecture of Venice
did.abstract: Photographic survey of buildings in Venice and the Veneto (Chioggia, Padua), mostly ecclesiastical architecture and sculpture, by the firm of Osvaldo Böhm, Fotografo Editore, Veneiza, S. Moise 1349-50. Photographs stamped on the reserve with Böhm inventory numbers, handwritten subject identification in Italian. Some duplicates. A few photographs are not stamped "Böhm" but are from otherwise identifiable sources including Alinari, Gabinetto Fotografico Nazionale (Rome), etc.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been transferred to Yale University. Further use of this material may be subject to the copyright laws of the United States (Title 17, United States Code) or to site license or other rights management terms or conditions.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Osvaldo Böhm Photograph Collection: Art and Architecture of Venice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographic survey of buildings in Venice and the Veneto (Chioggia, Padua), mostly ecclesiastical architecture and sculpture, by the firm of Osvaldo Böhm, Fotografo Editore, Veneiza, S. Moise 1349-50. Photographs stamped on the reserve with Böhm inventory numbers, handwritten subject identification in Italian. Some duplicates. A few photographs are not stamped "Böhm" but are from otherwise identifiable sources including Alinari, Gabinetto Fotografico Nazionale (Rome), etc. Total of 506 photographs

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: 4 boxes with 16 folders, some folders with additional sub-folders labeled by building.

did.unittitle: S. Maria delle Salute, architecture, (21 photographs)
S. Maria della Salute, interior sculpture, (73 photographs)
S. Maria della Salute, interior sculpture, (38 photographs)
Palazzo Ducale, mostly exterior sculpture, (74 photographs)
Scuola di S. Giorgio e Trionfo (4 photographs
Padua, S. Giustina, architecture and sculpture, (28 photographs)
